---
title: "BayesianAnalysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(faraway)
library(caret)
```

```{r BAC_calculator}
#standard formula 
shots <- readline(prompt = "Enter number of shots: ")
a = 0.6 * as.numeric(shots)
r = 0.68
w = 120
h = 2

bac = ((a * 5.14) / (w * r)) - (0.015 * h)
bac
```

```{r BAC_calculator2}
#change beta and r
#r can be modeled as a function of weight gender height age and water drank
shots <- readline(prompt = "Enter number of shots: ")
weight <- readline(prompt = "Enter weight: ")
height <- readline(prompt = "Enter height (in): ")
age <- readline(prompt = "Enter age: ")
a = 0.6 * as.numeric(shots)
r = 0.68
h = 2
beta = 0.015

#formula for men
r_update_men = (2.447 - 0.09156 * as.numeric(age) + 0.1074 * as.numeric(height) * 2.54 + 0.3362 * (as.numeric(weight) * 0.45359237)) / (as.numeric(weight) * 0.45359237)

#formula for women
r_update_women = (-2.097 + 0.1069 * as.numeric(height) * 2.54 + 0.2466 * (as.numeric(weight) * 0.45359237)) / (as.numeric(weight) * 0.45359237)

bac_update = ((a * 5.14) / (as.numeric(weight) * r_update_men)) - (beta * h)
bac_update
```

```{r CSV}
waterInfo <- read.csv("waterdata.csv")
waterInfo$percent <- waterInfo$watervol / waterInfo$weight
```

```{r}
IndependenceC <- aov(percent ~ gender, data = waterInfo)
summary.aov(IndependenceC)

waterInfoM <- waterInfo[waterInfo$gender == 1,]

model_M <- lm(percent ~ age + weight + height, data = waterInfoM)
summary(model_M)

waterInfoF <- waterInfo[waterInfo$gender == 2,]

model_F <- lm(percent ~ age + weight + height, data = waterInfoF)
summary(model_F)
```

```{r}
test <- data.frame(age = c(19),
                   weight = c(50),
                   height = c(164)
                   
)
predict(model_F, test)
```
